title: HotkeyRegistry
description: Global registry of keyboard bindings. Each entry pins a stable id, a default key sequence, the scope at which it fires, and (for screen-local entries) the owning screen package id. Pinned by docs/architecture/ui-hotkeys.md. The registry is the single source of truth — every interactions.md row that references a Hotkey id must resolve to an entry here.

id.description: Stable namespaced id. 'global.<action>' for engine-wide bindings (Esc, F10, ...); 'screen.<screenSlug>.<action>' for screen-local bindings.

defaultBinding.description: Default key sequence in canonical W3C KeyboardEvent.code form, e.g. 'Escape', 'Enter', 'F10', 'Control+Z', 'Control+Shift+Z'. Modifier order is Control, Alt, Shift, Meta.

scope.description: 'global' fires regardless of focus; 'screen' fires only on the active screen; 'modal' fires only when the matching modal id is the top of state.ui.modalStack.

screenId.description: Required when scope is 'screen' or 'modal'. Screen package id (e.g. '07-adventure-map' or '60-confirmation-dialog').

labelKey.description: Localization id for the human-readable label shown in keybinding UIs.

rebindable.description: False for hardcoded bindings (e.g. 'global.system-menu' on Esc) that the player cannot remap.
